mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side constraints and weakening manufacturing demand, leading to negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines coincide with positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of tightening periods, invalidating the supply-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 4.5 percent impose high financing costs on leveraged firms and reduce risk appetite, causing immediate negative market reactions over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of observations when FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with stable unemployment rates reflects healthy job creation without wage inflation pressure, supporting sustained corporate profitability and positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate strong consumer confidence and upcoming construction activity, which directly increases demand for materials and services, pushing equity prices upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows, or HOUST increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns at a 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks and weak factory orders, triggering rapid negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 40% of instances when INDPRO declines during recovery phases, breaking the proposed causal link.
